input.content:
---
name: Firewall rule tuning patterns
description: Lessons from iteratively tuning trust-firewall.yaml to reduce permission noise without losing safety
type: feedback
---

When adding tools to the trust_circle, categorize accurately — don't label `mkdir` as "read-only" just because it's safe. Give it its own rule with an honest reason ("non-destructive").

Common CC tools that need explicit trust_circle rules to avoid noise: TaskCreate/TaskUpdate/TaskGet/TaskList/TaskOutput/TaskStop, Skill, ToolSearch, EnterPlanMode/ExitPlanMode, EnterWorktree/ExitWorktree, SendMessage, AskUserQuestion, NotebookEdit. All are internal CC orchestration with zero side effects.

`push.*origin` in blocked tier is too broad — it blocks legitimate branch pushes. Use `^git push\b` in restricted (ask) and rely on `--force` in blocked to catch force pushes specifically.

**Why:** User was getting prompted for every TaskUpdate, mkdir, Skill invocation, etc. — dozens of unnecessary prompts per session. The blocked push rule prevented `git push -u origin feature/xyz` entirely.
**How to apply:** When adding new rules, think about what the default (ask) catches unnecessarily. Test with real CC sessions, not just unit tests.

reason: No matching rule — falling back to default
